title: DOM-based XSS
description: A page that uses JavaScript to display a welcome message from the URL hash.

stages.name: Find the vulnerability
stages.instruction: Explain how the vulnerability occurs in the client-side code.
stages.target: The script takes data from 'window.location.hash' and writes it directly to 'innerHTML' without sanitization.

stages.name: Exploit it
stages.instruction: Provide a URL hash that would execute JavaScript when the page is loaded.

stages.name: Patch it
stages.instruction: What JavaScript property should be used instead of 'innerHTML' to prevent this?

stages.name: Explain it
stages.instruction: Why does the server not see the payload in a DOM-based XSS attack using the URL hash?
stages.target: The fragment identifier (part after #) is not sent to the server by the browser.

hints: The vulnerability is in the JavaScript code, not the server.
The part of the URL after '#' is called the hash.
